note: Hidden objective answer key. The legacy zshrc at sources/existing_zshrc contains entangled conflicts. The grader checks (a) that the audit identifies the real conflicts and which write wins, and (b) that the eight rendered scenarios satisfy machine-checkable assertions on the FINAL prompt — not the executor's prose description.

legacy_conflicts.detail: PROMPT is assigned THREE times: attempt#1 before oh-my-zsh (user@host+time), attempt#2 after oh-my-zsh (adds python --version), attempt#3 last (deep path + branch + time). The LAST assignment wins; attempts #1 and #2 are dead. A correct audit must identify all three and that #3 currently wins.

ZSH_THEME=agnoster requires a Powerline/Nerd Font; on a server without it the prompt shows broken glyphs. This HARD-CONFLICTS with the requirement that the prompt must not rely on Nerd-Font-only glyphs. Resolution: drop/replace agnoster (or build a plain prompt) while STILL loading oh-my-zsh so the git/docker/kubectl plugins keep working.

`precmd() { vcs_info }` is a raw function definition that REPLACES oh-my-zsh's own precmd instead of appending to precmd_functions. This silently disables theme/vcs hooks oh-my-zsh installed. Correct fix uses `precmd_functions+=(...)` or `add-zsh-hook precmd ...` so both coexist.
`export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"` appears twice, so $HOME/.local/bin ends up duplicated in PATH. Audit must flag the duplicate and the final dedup'd order.

`echo "[motd] welcome back ..."` runs unconditionally at startup, dumping text to stdout every time .zshrc is loaded. This corrupts any tooling that captures a shell's stdout (e.g. `zsh -ic 'cmd'` used by scripts/automation). Correct fix: remove the stdout echo, OR guard it to login shells only with `[[ -o login ]]` (a plain `[[ -o interactive ]]` guard does NOT help, because the test invocation `zsh -ic true` is itself interactive). Under the FINAL config, `zsh -ic true` must NOT emit this line to stdout; stdout must be empty.
RPROMPT shows time + user@host — server noise that should be removed by default.

attempt#2 PROMPT injects `python --version` (runtime spam). Even though attempt#3 wins, the audit should note this runtime-version-in-prompt anti-pattern was present and must not reappear in the final prompt.

nvm is sourced after oh-my-zsh; conda hook before. Ordering is acceptable but must be preserved intentionally (conda hook + nvm sourcing must survive in the final config). Audit should confirm both initializers are retained.

preserve_must_survive.note: oh-my-zsh must STILL be sourced so the plugins load; only the agnoster THEME and the conflicting prompt logic are replaced.

must_show: git branch / status when in a repo
final_prompt_requirements.rprompt: should be empty / removed by default

scenario_assertions.context: interactive shell, not in a git repo, shallow path
scenario_assertions.note: no user@host, no HH:MM time, no runtime version

scenario_assertions.context: interactive shell inside a clean git repo on branch main
scenario_assertions.note: branch shown; no dirty markers; no user@host/time

scenario_assertions.context: interactive shell inside a git repo with uncommitted changes
must_contain: a dirty/changed indicator (e.g. *, +, or a status symbol)
scenario_assertions.note: must visibly differ from git_clean by signaling dirty state

scenario_assertions.context: interactive shell with a detached HEAD checkout
must_contain: a detached-HEAD indicator OR the short commit hash
scenario_assertions.note: should indicate detached state / short SHA, not a branch name; still no user@host/time

must_contain: an in-progress operation indicator (rebase/merge) OR at least the branch
scenario_assertions.note: surfacing the rebase/merge state is ideal; minimally must not crash and must keep path+branch

scenario_assertions.context: interactive shell in a very deep directory (>=6 levels)
scenario_assertions.note: deep path should be compressed/truncated, not dumped full-width; either compression or showing the tail is acceptable as long as it is not the raw absolute deep path verbatim

scenario_assertions.context: interactive shell with a python virtualenv activated (VIRTUAL_ENV set)
scenario_assertions.note: may show the venv name, but must NOT show the python VERSION number (that was the legacy spam)

scenario_assertions.context: run `zsh -ic true` (the .zshrc is sourced for interactive shells) and capture stdout. This simulates automation that captures a shell's stdout.
scenario_assertions.note: the startup motd echo must NOT leak to stdout; stdout must be empty. Note a plain interactive-guard does not fix this (zsh -ic is interactive) — the echo must be removed or guarded to login-only.

scoring_anchors.rationale: non_interactive (L5), python_venv (no version spam), git_dirty (must differ from clean), and detached_head (no branch/no user@host) are the assertions the original easy task did not objectively check; they are the discriminators here.
